brief_title: Long-Acting Cabotegravir Plus Rilpivirine Injections in Treatment-Naive Adults with HIV-1
official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study Comparing Long-Acting Injectable Cabotegravir Plus Rilpivirine to Standard Oral Antiretroviral Therapy in Treatment-Naive Adults with HIV-1 Infection
interventions: Cabotegravir 600mg IM injection monthly plus Rilpivirine 900mg IM injection monthly
brief_summary: This Phase 3 study compares the efficacy and safety of long-acting injectable cabotegravir plus rilpivirine administered monthly versus standard oral antiretroviral therapy in treatment-naive adults with HIV-1. The primary endpoint is proportion of participants with HIV-1 RNA < 50 copies/mL at week 48.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 18-65 years

2. Documented HIV-1 infection by licensed diagnostic test

3. Antiretroviral treatment-naive (no prior ART exposure)

4. HIV-1 RNA >= 1000 copies/mL at screening

5. CD4+ T-cell count > 200 cells/μL

6. No documented resistance to integrase inhibitors or rilpivirine by genotype

7. Hepatitis B surface antigen negative

8. eGFR >= 50 mL/min by CKD-EPI equation

9. ALT <= 5x upper limit of normal

10. Willing to receive intramuscular injections monthly

11. Women of childbearing potential must use effective contraception

12. Written informed consent
raw_exclusion_text:
1. HIV-2 co-infection

2. Active or untreated opportunistic infection at screening

3. Active hepatitis B or C co-infection requiring treatment

4. Chronic hepatitis B (HBsAg positive)

5. History of malignancy within past 5 years (except cutaneous Kaposi's sarcoma, basal cell carcinoma)

6. Current use of drugs known to prolong QTc interval

7. Known hypersensitivity to cabotegravir or rilpivirine

8. Pregnant or breastfeeding

9. Active substance use disorder that may interfere with study compliance

10. Participation in another investigational drug study within 30 days

inclusion_criteria.text: Documented HIV-1 infection by licensed diagnostic test
No documented resistance to integrase inhibitors or rilpivirine by genotype
Willing to receive intramuscular injections monthly
Women of childbearing potential must use effective contraception

exclusion_criteria.text: Active or untreated opportunistic infection at screening
Active hepatitis B or C co-infection requiring treatment
History of malignancy within past 5 years (except cutaneous Kaposi's sarcoma, basal cell carcinoma)
Current use of drugs known to prolong QTc interval
Known hypersensitivity to cabotegravir or rilpivirine
Active substance use disorder that may interfere with study compliance
Participation in another investigational drug study within 30 days

sites.facility: Emory University HIV Clinical Trials Unit
